name: Aiven MCP Server (Official)
what_it_does: Official Aiven MCP server enabling AI agents to manage Aiven's managed cloud data infrastructure — provisioning and operating Kafka, PostgreSQL, MySQL, OpenSearch, Redis, Cassandra, and other managed database services across AWS, GCP, and Azure.
use_cases: Provisioning managed Kafka or PostgreSQL services from infrastructure agents
Monitoring service health and resource utilization from ops agents
Managing database connection pools and credentials from security agents
Scaling services up or down based on load from autoscaling agents
Listing available service plans and regions for cost optimization agents
Configuring service integrations between Aiven components
not_for: Teams managing their own cloud databases directly (use cloud provider MCP)
Teams using non-Aiven managed database providers
Direct SQL query execution (use database-specific MCP for that)
best_when: An agent needs to provision or manage Aiven's managed cloud data services — especially for multi-cloud database infrastructure.
avoid_when: You're managing databases directly on AWS RDS, GCP Cloud SQL, or Azure SQL — use those providers' MCPs instead.

auth.notes: Aiven authentication tokens with user or service account scope. OAuth2 for user-delegated access. Project and organization-level access control.

pricing.paid_tiers: Service-based: varies by service type, size, and cloud region
pricing.notes: Consumption-based pricing per managed service. Free trial with credits. MCP server is open source.

performance.rate_limits: API rate limits not prominently documented

known_agent_gotchas: Project name is required for most operations — discover available projects first
Service creation is async — agents must poll for service to reach RUNNING state
Service termination is permanent and immediate — agents must confirm before executing
Different services (Kafka, PG, etc.) have different configuration schemas
Cloud and plan selection affects pricing significantly — agents should be cost-aware
VPC peering setup requires additional network configuration steps
agent_readiness.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. Auth tokens with org/project scope. SOC 2, ISO 27001, HIPAA, GDPR. Aiven manages underlying infrastructure security.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. Auth tokens with org/project scope. SOC 2, ISO 27001, HIPAA, GDPR. Aiven manages cloud infrastructure security.
